question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified] Can an unprivileged maker call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) between the moment a taker's multicall encodes the take calldata and the moment take() executes on-chain, under the precondition that the maker has a live Merkle-root-ratified offer tree and a taker has already approved token spend, triggering the call sequence [maker calls setIsRootRatified(root, false) -> taker's take() calls isRatified() -> require(isRootRatified[maker][root]) reverts with NotRatified], violating the invariant that a ratified offer must remain fillable until its expiry or cancellation, causing scoped impact: taker's pre-approved assets are locked in pending approval and the take is permanently blocked for that root? Proof idea: fuzz the block ordering of setIsRootRatified vs take() and assert that a root revocation after offer signing but before take execution causes take to revert even though the offer was validly ratified at signing time.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.setIsAuthorized] Can an authorized operator (via isAuthorized[maker][operator] == true) call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) on behalf of the maker without the maker's knowledge, under the precondition that the maker has authorized the operator for any purpose and has live offers in the revoked root, triggering the call sequence [operator calls setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) -> all pending takes against that root revert with NotRatified], violating the invariant that only the maker should be able to cancel their own offers, causing scoped impact: all takers holding valid Merkle proofs for that root are griefed and cannot fill the offer? Proof idea: unit test where operator != maker calls setIsRootRatified and assert all subsequent take() calls revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can a taker submit a valid Merkle proof with leafIndex set to a value where leafIndex >> proof.length != 0, under the precondition that the offer is otherwise valid and the root is ratified, triggering the call sequence [take() -> isRatified() -> HashLib.isLeaf() -> require(leafIndex >> proof.length == 0) reverts with LeafIndexOutOfRange], violating the invariant that a valid offer with a correct root should always be fillable, causing scoped impact: taker is griefed if they miscalculate leafIndex for a large tree, permanently blocking their take? Proof idea: fuzz leafIndex and proof.length pairs and assert that any leafIndex >= 2^proof.length causes revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a buy offer with units=1 at a tick where buyerPrice < WAD such that buyerAssets = mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD) = 0, under the precondition that the offer uses maxAssets-based consumption tracking (maxAssets > 0), triggering the call sequence [take(offer, ratifierData, units=1, ...) -> consumed[maker][group] += 0 -> require(0 <= maxAssets) passes -> buyer credit increases by 1 unit -> 0 assets transferred], violating the invariant that consumed offer capacity must increase with each fill, causing scoped impact: attacker can call take() with units=1 indefinitely without consuming the offer's maxAssets budget, griefing the maker by generating unbounded credit units at zero cost? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, WAD/buyerPrice - 1] and assert consumed[maker][group] does not increase while buyerPos.credit increases.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a sell offer with units=1 at a tick where sellerPrice < WAD such that sellerAssets = mulDivUp(1, sellerPrice, WAD) = 0 and buyerAssets = mulDivUp(1, buyerPrice, WAD) = 0, under the precondition that the offer uses maxAssets-based consumption tracking, triggering the call sequence [take(offer, ratifierData, units=1, ...) -> consumed[maker][group] += 0 -> require(0 <= maxAssets) passes -> seller debt increases by 1 unit -> 0 assets transferred], violating the invariant that each fill must transfer assets proportional to units, causing scoped impact: maker's debt increases by dust units without receiving any loan token assets, griefing the maker-seller? Proof idea: unit test with tick near minimum and units=1, assert sellerAssets=0 and seller debt increases.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker repeatedly fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer with dust units (units=1) to exhaust the maxUnits budget without providing meaningful liquidity, under the precondition that the offer uses maxUnits-based consumption and the ratifier root is ratified, triggering the call sequence [N calls to take(offer, ratifierData, 1, ...) -> consumed[maker][group] increments by 1 each time -> after maxUnits calls, offer is exhausted], violating the invariant that offer capacity should only be consumed by economically meaningful fills, causing scoped impact: maker's offer is griefed into exhaustion with negligible economic activity, blocking legitimate takers? Proof idea: fuzz N and assert that N=maxUnits dust fills exhaust the offer before any single meaningful fill can occur.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can a taker construct ratifierData with a valid root and proof for offer A but submit it alongside a different offer B that hashes to the same leaf, under the precondition that both offers share the same Merkle tree and the root is ratified, triggering the call sequence [take(offerB, ratifierData_for_A, ...) -> hashOffer(offerB) -> isLeaf(root, hashOffer(offerB), leafIndex, proof) -> returns false -> require fails with InvalidProof], violating the invariant that only the exact offer in the tree should be fillable, causing scoped impact: taker is griefed if they accidentally submit mismatched offer/proof pairs, permanently blocking their take? Proof idea: differential test comparing hashOffer outputs for offers differing by one field and assert proof non-transferability.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer at block.timestamp == offer.market.maturity (the exact maturity boundary), under the precondition that the offer is a sell offer (debt-increasing) and block.timestamp == maturity, triggering the call sequence [take() -> timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(maturity, block.timestamp) = 0 -> sellerDebtIncrease > 0 -> require(block.timestamp <= maturity || sellerDebtIncrease == 0) -> passes since block.timestamp == maturity], violating the invariant that debt must not increase after maturity, causing scoped impact: a borrower can increase debt at exactly the maturity timestamp, creating debt that immediately becomes post-maturity liquidatable? Proof idea: warp to block.timestamp == maturity and assert take() with sellerDebtIncrease > 0 succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified sell offer at block.timestamp == maturity - 1 (one second before maturity), creating debt, then have that debt become immediately post-maturity liquidatable at block.timestamp == maturity, under the precondition that the borrower has no collateral, triggering the call sequence [take at maturity-1 -> debt created -> warp to maturity -> liquidate(postMaturityMode=true) -> borrower liquidated], violating the invariant that a borrower should have time to repay before post-maturity liquidation, causing scoped impact: borrower's funds are seized immediately after maturity with no grace period? Proof idea: state test warping from maturity-1 to maturity and asserting immediate liquidatability.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.supplyCollateral / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a borrower be griefed into hitting the MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) bitmap limit by an attacker who supplies dust amounts of 16 different collateral tokens on behalf of the borrower (via isAuthorized), under the precondition that the borrower has authorized the attacker and the market has 128 collateral types, triggering the call sequence [attacker calls supplyCollateral(market, i, 1, borrower) for i in 0..15 -> collateralBitmap has 16 bits set -> borrower's next supplyCollateral for index 16 reverts with TooManyActivatedCollaterals], violating the invariant that a borrower should be able to manage their own collateral composition, causing scoped impact: borrower cannot add new collateral types, potentially preventing them from maintaining health or taking new offers? Proof idea: unit test supplying 1 wei of 16 different collaterals and assert 17th supply reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.withdrawCollateral / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a borrower who has 16 activated collaterals (bitmap full) be unable to withdraw a dust collateral amount (1 wei) from one slot to free up a bitmap bit, under the precondition that the borrower has debt and the withdrawal of 1 wei from a slot with 1 wei balance would set newCollateral=0 and clear the bit, triggering the call sequence [withdrawCollateral(market, i, 1, borrower, receiver) -> newCollateral=0 -> clearBit -> isHealthy check -> passes -> bit cleared], violating the invariant that bitmap management should always allow collateral withdrawal to free slots, causing scoped impact: if isHealthy reverts due to oracle issues on other collaterals, the borrower cannot free bitmap slots? Proof idea: fuzz oracle revert conditions and assert withdrawCollateral reverts when any activated collateral oracle reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's group is shared with another offer, and a dust fill of the first offer partially consumes the group budget, under the precondition that both offers share the same group bytes32 and the group budget is near-exhausted, triggering the call sequence [take(offer1, ratifierData1, dustUnits, ...) -> consumed[maker][group] += dustUnits -> take(offer2, ratifierData2, largeUnits, ...) -> consumed[maker][group] + largeUnits > maxUnits -> reverts with ConsumedUnits], violating the invariant that group budget should be consumed proportionally across offers, causing scoped impact: a dust fill of one offer in a group blocks all remaining fills of other offers in the same group? Proof idea: fuzz dust fill amounts and assert group budget exhaustion blocks legitimate fills.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.take] Can a maker ratify a new root (root2) that contains a modified version of an offer (same group, higher maxUnits) after a partial fill has already consumed some of the group budget under root1, under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] > 0 from fills under root1, triggering the call sequence [fills under root1 consume N units -> maker ratifies root2 with same offer but maxUnits = M > N -> take under root2 -> consumed[maker][group] += units -> require(consumed <= M) passes], violating the invariant that consumed group budget should persist across root rotations, causing scoped impact: maker can effectively reset or expand offer capacity by rotating roots, potentially overfilling their intended exposure? Proof idea: state test rotating roots and asserting consumed tracking persists correctly.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified buy offer with units just below the rounding threshold such that buyerAssets rounds down to exactly offer.maxAssets - 1, then fill again with units=1 such that the second fill's buyerAssets rounds to 0, under the precondition that maxAssets > 0 and buyerPrice < WAD, triggering the call sequence [fill1: consumed += maxAssets-1 -> fill2: consumed += 0 -> require(maxAssets-1 <= maxAssets) passes -> buyer gets 1 more credit unit for free], violating the invariant that total filled assets must not exceed maxAssets, causing scoped impact: maker's offer is overfilled by rounding, griefing the maker? Proof idea: fuzz units pairs and assert sum of buyerAssets across fills never exceeds maxAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer at a settlement fee breakpoint boundary (e.g., timeToMaturity == 1 days exactly) where the piecewise linear interpolation in settlementFee() produces a different fee than at timeToMaturity == 1 days - 1, under the precondition that the offer's tick is near the minimum viable price, triggering the call sequence [take at timeToMaturity=1 days -> settlementFee uses cbp0/cbp1 boundary -> buyerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee -> if buyerPrice < 0 revert], violating the invariant that settlement fee must not exceed offer price, causing scoped impact: taker's take reverts at fee breakpoint boundaries, temporarily freezing their ability to fill the offer? Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity at each breakpoint boundary and assert settlementFee continuity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified buy offer where the settlement fee equals the offer price (offerPrice == settlementFee), under the precondition that the offer tick is at the minimum and timeToMaturity is near zero, triggering the call sequence [take() -> sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee = 0 -> sellerAssets = mulDivDown(units, 0, WAD) = 0 -> buyerAssets = mulDivUp(units, settlementFee, WAD) > 0 -> payer must transfer buyerAssets to protocol with 0 going to seller], violating the invariant that sellers must receive assets for their credit, causing scoped impact: seller receives 0 assets while buyer pays full settlement fee, effectively freezing seller's expected proceeds? Proof idea: unit test with offerPrice == settlementFee and assert sellerAssets == 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can a taker submit ratifierData with proof.length == 0 (single-leaf tree), under the precondition that the root equals hashOffer(offer) and isRootRatified[maker][root] == true, triggering the call sequence [isRatified() -> decode(ratifierData) -> leafIndex=0, proof=[] -> isLeaf(root, hashOffer(offer), 0, []) -> require(0 >> 0 == 0) passes -> currentHash = hashOffer(offer) -> return currentHash == root -> true], violating no invariant (this is valid), causing scoped impact: verify that a single-offer tree with proof.length=0 is correctly handled and does not allow any other offer to be ratified under the same root? Proof idea: unit test with proof=[] and assert only the exact offer whose hash equals root passes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where offer.maxAssets > 0 and offer.buy == true, and the rounding of buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) causes consumed to increase by less than units would suggest, under the precondition that buyerPrice is slightly below WAD, triggering the call sequence [take(units=K) -> buyerAssets = floor(K * buyerPrice / WAD) < K -> consumed += buyerAssets -> require(consumed <= maxAssets) -> passes even when total units filled > maxAssets/buyerPrice], violating the invariant that total units filled must not exceed the offer's intended capacity, causing scoped impact: maker's offer is filled for more units than intended due to rounding, griefing the maker-buyer? Proof idea: fuzz buyerPrice in [WAD/2, WAD-1] and assert total units filled never exceeds ceil(maxAssets * WAD / buyerPrice).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer at block.timestamp == offer.expiry (exact expiry boundary), under the precondition that the offer has expiry set to a specific block timestamp, triggering the call sequence [take() -> require(block.timestamp <= offer.expiry) -> passes at equality -> offer is filled], violating the invariant that expired offers must not be fillable, causing scoped impact: an offer that the maker intended to expire at a specific time can still be filled at that exact timestamp, potentially griefing the maker? Proof idea: warp to block.timestamp == offer.expiry and assert take() succeeds, then warp to expiry+1 and assert revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where offer.start == block.timestamp (exact start boundary), under the precondition that the offer has start set to the current block timestamp, triggering the call sequence [take() -> require(block.timestamp >= offer.start) -> passes at equality -> offer is filled], violating no invariant (this is valid), causing scoped impact: verify that the start boundary is inclusive and that a taker cannot fill before start by manipulating block.timestamp? Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp in [start-1, start, start+1] and assert revert only at start-1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified / Midnight.multicall] Can an attacker use multicall to atomically call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false) and then take() in the same transaction, under the precondition that the attacker is authorized for the maker, triggering the call sequence [multicall([setIsRootRatified(maker, root, false), take(offer, ratifierData, ...)]) -> setIsRootRatified executes -> isRootRatified[maker][root] = false -> take() -> isRatified() -> require(isRootRatified[maker][root]) reverts], violating the invariant that multicall should not allow atomic griefing of maker offers, causing scoped impact: authorized operator can atomically revoke and block a take in the same transaction, griefing any taker who submitted a transaction in the same block? Proof idea: unit test multicall with revocation followed by take and assert revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer's collateral bitmap for the seller is at MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) and the take would increase the seller's debt (sellerDebtIncrease > 0), under the precondition that the seller already has 16 activated collaterals, triggering the call sequence [take() -> sellerDebtIncrease > 0 -> isHealthy(market, id, seller) -> iterates all 16 collaterals -> health check passes -> take succeeds], violating no invariant (bitmap limit only applies to supplyCollateral), causing scoped impact: verify that a seller at bitmap limit can still take sell offers and that the health check correctly iterates all 16 collaterals without gas exhaustion? Proof idea: fuzz collateral count up to 16 and assert isHealthy gas cost scales linearly.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a liquidator be griefed when trying to liquidate a borrower who has 16 activated collaterals (bitmap full) and one of the collateral oracles reverts, under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy and the reverting oracle is not the liquidated collateral, triggering the call sequence [liquidate(market, collateralIndex, ...) -> iterate collateralBitmap -> IOracle(collateralParam.oracle).price() reverts for some i -> liquidate reverts], violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: borrower's position cannot be liquidated due to a reverting oracle on a non-liquidated collateral, temporarily freezing the liquidation? Proof idea: mock one oracle to revert and assert liquidate() reverts even for unhealthy positions.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the offer uses reduceOnly=true and the maker's credit is exactly 0, under the precondition that the maker is a buyer with reduceOnly=true and buyerCreditIncrease > 0, triggering the call sequence [take() -> buyerCreditIncrease = zeroFloorSub(units, buyerPos.debt) -> if buyerPos.debt == 0 then buyerCreditIncrease = units > 0 -> require(!offer.reduceOnly || buyerCreditIncrease == 0) reverts with MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased], violating the invariant that reduceOnly offers should be fillable when they reduce debt, causing scoped impact: a maker's reduceOnly offer is permanently blocked when their debt reaches 0, freezing the offer? Proof idea: unit test with reduceOnly=true and maker debt=0 and assert take reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: Midnight.take / SetterRatifier.isRatified] Can a taker fill a Merkle-root-ratified offer where the market's lossFactor == type(uint128).max, under the precondition that the market has experienced total bad debt loss, triggering the call sequence [take() -> require(_marketState.lossFactor < type(uint128).max) reverts with MarketLossFactorMaxedOut], violating the invariant that a market with maxed loss factor should still allow exits, causing scoped impact: all takes in the market are permanently blocked, freezing lender credit and borrower debt? Proof idea: force lossFactor to type(uint128).max via repeated bad debt and assert take() reverts.
